leibel2023_disruption_index.title: What do we know about the disruption index in scientometrics? An overview of the literature
leibel2023_disruption_index.research_question: What are the properties, validity, and limitations of the disruption index (DI1) and its variants in scientometrics?
leibel2023_disruption_index.data_source: Literature review of published studies on the disruption index and its variants
conclusion_claims: The disruption index (DI1) and its variants have been widely applied in scientometrics, but their validity and limitations need further investigation.
Modified index variants, such as DI5, show higher degrees of convergent validity than DI1.
Disruption indices suffer from inconsistency, time-sensitive biases, and several data-induced biases.
More research is needed on the validity of disruption scores and a more precise understanding of disruption as a theoretical construct before the indices can be used in research evaluation practice.
limitations: The literature on convergent validity is not entirely conclusive.
Disruption indices suffer from inconsistency, time-sensitive biases, and several data-induced biases.
The theoretical construct of disruption is not yet fully understood.

result_claims.value: some modified index variants (e.g., DI5) show higher degrees of convergent validity than DI1
disruption indices suffer from several data-induced biases
disruption indices suffer from time-sensitive biases

partial_results.note: The literature on convergent validity is not entirely conclusive.

funk2017_dynamic_network.title: A Dynamic Network Measure of Technological Change
funk2017_dynamic_network.research_question: How do new inventions reshape the network of interlinked technologies, and what factors influence whether they consolidate or destabilize the status quo?
conclusion_claims: Federal research funding encourages universities to produce more destabilizing inventions, while deeper commercial ties lead to the production of technologies that consolidate the status quo.
The CDt and mCDt indexes provide a novel way to quantify the impact of new technologies on prior technologies, offering insights that are not detectable with traditional citation-based measures.
limitations: The study focuses on university-related patents and may not generalize to other types of organizations.
The measurement of commercial engagement and federal funding may be subject to biases or incomplete data.

result_claims.value: Higher for universities with federal funding
Higher for universities with commercial engagement

partial_results.note: Forward-citation-based measures do not distinguish between destabilizing and consolidating inventions, leading to ambiguity in results.

petersen2023_disruption_index.title: The disruption index is biased by citation inflation
petersen2023_disruption_index.research_question: Is the observed decline in the disruption index over time a true reflection of reduced innovation or an artifact of citation inflation?
petersen2023_disruption_index.data_source: Clarivate Analytics Web of Science, Microsoft Academic Graph (MAG)
conclusion_claims: The disruption index is systematically biased by citation inflation and is unsuitable for cross-temporal analysis.
Citation inflation is driven by increasing reference list lengths and self-citations, which increase triadic closure in citation networks.
limitations: The analysis is based on descriptive statistics and does not include formal statistical testing.
The paper does not propose an alternative to the disruption index that accounts for citation inflation.

partial_results.note: The decline in CD is attributed to citation inflation rather than a true decline in innovation.

ke2023_network_based_normalized_impact.title: A network-based normalized impact measure reveals successful periods of scientific discovery across disciplines
ke2023_network_based_normalized_impact.research_question: How can we develop an unbiased citation normalization method to compare scientific impact across disciplines and time periods?
ke2023_network_based_normalized_impact.data_source: Web of Science, Scopus, and other citation databases (not explicitly named but implied through methodology)
conclusion_claims: The proposed normalized citation measure (ˆC) provides an unbiased indicator of scientific impact across disciplines and time.
The method reveals high-impact research in diverse, smaller disciplines that are often overshadowed by highly cited biomedical sciences.
limitations: The method relies on the availability of comprehensive citation data, which may not be accessible for all fields or time periods.
The cocitation-based normalization assumes that cocited papers are topically related, which may not always be accurate.

result_claims.value: follows a universal distribution across disciplines and time
diverse fields such as geosciences, radiology, and optics are identified as sources of high-impact research

partial_results.note: The paper compares ˆC with RCR but does not provide detailed statistical significance for the comparison.

bikard2013_exploring_tradeoffs.title: Exploring Tradeoffs in the Organization of Scientific Work: Collaboration and Scientific Reward
bikard2013_exploring_tradeoffs.research_question: How do scientists balance the benefits of collaboration (e.g., higher quality outputs) with the costs of credit allocation, and what factors influence their collaborative choices?
bikard2013_exploring_tradeoffs.data_source: Academic publications of 661 faculty scientists at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).
conclusion_claims: Collaboration is associated with higher quality scientific outputs (e.g., more citations) and increased fractional credit for individual scientists.
Cross-departmental collaborations tend to produce higher quality papers at a lower productivity cost compared to within-departmental collaborations.
limitations: The study is limited to a single institution (MIT), which may not be representative of the broader scientific community.
The paper does not fully quantify the mechanisms of credit allocation in collaborative work.

result_claims.value: higher quality papers at lower productivity cost
low quality gain and high productivity loss

partial_results.note: The paper suggests that credit may be disproportionately allocated in collaborative work, but the exact mechanism is not fully quantified.

park2023_disruptive.title: Papers and patents are becoming less disruptive over time
park2023_disruptive.research_question: Are papers and patents becoming less disruptive over time, and if so, what factors might explain this trend?
park2023_disruptive.data_source: Web of Science (WoS), United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) Patents View database, JSTOR, American Physical Society corpus, Microsoft Academic Graph, PubMed
conclusion_claims: Papers and patents are becoming less disruptive over time, as indicated by a decline in the CD5 index.
The decline in disruptiveness is not due to changes in the quality of published science, citation practices, or field-specific factors.
limitations: The study relies on citation data, which may not fully capture the impact or novelty of a paper or patent.
The CD5 index is a novel metric, and its interpretation may be subject to debate.

result_claims.value: decreased from 0.52 in 1945 to 0.04 in 2010 for 'social sciences'
decreased from 0.30 in 1980 to 0.06 in 2010 for 'computers and communications'

partial_results.note: The rate of decline is greatest in the earlier parts of the time series and appears to stabilize for patents between 2000 and 2005.

arts2021_natural_language_processing.title: Natural language processing to identify the creation and impact of new technologies in patent text: Code, data, and new measures
arts2021_natural_language_processing.research_question: Can natural language processing techniques improve the identification of the creation and impact of new technologies in patents compared to traditional metrics?
arts2021_natural_language_processing.data_source: USPTO, patent claims research dataset, PATSTAT
conclusion_claims: NLP-based measures outperform traditional metrics based on patent classification and citations in identifying the creation and impact of new technologies in patents.
New keyword combinations and their reuse by later patents show the strongest discriminatory power to classify both patents linked to prestigious awards and rejected patents.
limitations: The paper does not provide a detailed analysis of the limitations of the NLP-based measures.
The validation is based on case-control studies and may not generalize to all patents.

partial_results.note: The weak correlation suggests that NLP-based measures capture different aspects of novelty and impact compared to traditional metrics.

schaper2025_frontier_scientists.title: Not like the others: Frontier scientists for inventive performance
schaper2025_frontier_scientists.research_question: Do corporate inventors who are also frontier scientists produce more impactful inventions compared to other inventors, and what mechanisms explain this impact premium?
schaper2025_frontier_scientists.data_source: PubMed-indexed scientific articles, U.S. corporate biomedical patents, and disambiguation data from Torvik (2018) and Li et al. (2014)
conclusion_claims: Inventions by frontier authors have a significant impact premium in terms of technology impact, likelihood of becoming technology hits, and private value.
The impact premium is strongest in young, scaled-up biopharmaceutical firms and when frontier authors are internally employed.
limitations: The study is limited to the biomedical industry and U.S. patents, so the findings may not generalize to other industries or countries.
The paper does not fully disentangle the causal mechanisms behind the impact premium of frontier-author patents.

partial_results.note: While frontier-author patents are more likely to reference frontier science, the impact premium is not solely explained by this factor.

gebhart2023_mathematical_framework.title: A Mathematical Framework for Citation Disruption
gebhart2023_mathematical_framework.research_question: How can citation disruption be formalized using network centrality to unify and generalize existing disruption measures?
gebhart2023_mathematical_framework.data_source: Real citation data (not specific datasets mentioned)
conclusion_claims: The proposed centrality-based framework unifies and generalizes existing citation disruption measures.
Centrality-based disruption measures outperform conventional metrics in identifying future award-winning scientific innovations.
limitations: The paper does not specify the exact datasets used for empirical validation.
The theoretical framework is not tested across a wide range of scientific domains.

result_claims.value: higher discernment of future award-winning innovations
less discernment of future award-winning innovations compared to centrality-based measures

partial_results.note: The paper does not provide specific numerical results but validates the theoretical framework through empirical analysis.

ke2023_sleeping_beauties.title: Defining and Identifying Sleeping Beauties in Science
ke2023_sleeping_beauties.research_question: How common are Sleeping Beauties in science, and what is the nature of their delayed recognition?
ke2023_sleeping_beauties.data_source: Not explicitly mentioned, but it is implied to be a large-scale multidisciplinary bibliographic dataset.
conclusion_claims: The SB phenomenon is not exceptional but rather part of a continuous spectrum of delayed recognition.
The SB phenomenon is more apparent in multidisciplinary datasets, where papers achieve delayed importance in different fields.
limitations: The study does not explicitly mention the specific bibliographic database used.
The analysis is descriptive and does not provide a causal explanation for the SB phenomenon.

partial_results.note: The study suggests that SBs achieve delayed importance in disciplines different from their original publication field, but the exact mechanisms are not fully explored.

bentley2023_disruption.title: IS DISRUPTION DECREASING, OR IS IT ACCELERATING?
bentley2023_disruption.research_question: Is the observed decline in disruption over time a true decline or an artifact of exponential growth in scientific output?
bentley2023_disruption.data_source: Millions of scientific publications and patents from the dataset used in [1], as well as English-language books data from the Google Books Ngram database.
conclusion_claims: There is little evidence that disruption is decreasing; the citation-weighted disruption index has been close to its expected neutral value over the last fifty years.
Disruption may actually be increasing in the current age of digital publishing, as indicated by a modest but consistent growth in the weighted disruption index since 2000.
limitations: The analysis relies on the same dataset as the original study [1], which may have its own biases or limitations.
The paper does not fully explain why information and publication growth occurs exponentially.

partial_results.note: The CD5 metric shows a decline over time, but this is attributed to the exponential growth in publications and citations rather than a true decline in disruption.

traag2025_citation_models.title: Science of science—Citation models and research evaluation
traag2025_citation_models.research_question: How can citation models and research evaluation be integrated to better understand the role of citations in science and their implications for research quality assessment?
traag2025_citation_models.data_source: Web of Science, American Physics Society (APS), PACS codes, journal subject categories
conclusion_claims: Citation distributions are highly skewed and can be modeled using power laws or lognormals, depending on the normalization method.
Citation-based indicators are influenced by unobservable research quality and require causal reasoning to interpret correctly.
limitations: The unobservable nature of research quality limits the conclusions that can be drawn from citation-based indicators.
Field-specific differences in citation patterns challenge the universality of citation models.

partial_results.note: Some studies suggest universality, while others find field-specific differences, indicating uncertainty in the universality claim.

obadage2023_citations_reproducibility.title: Can Citations Tell Us About a Paper’s Reproducibility? A Case Study of Machine Learning Papers
obadage2023_citations_reproducibility.research_question: Can citation context sentiment be used as a signal to infer the reproducibility of a paper in the field of machine learning?
obadage2023_citations_reproducibility.data_source: Semantic Scholar Graph API (S2GA), Machine Learning Reproducibility Challenges (MLRC), and manually labeled citation contexts.
conclusion_claims: There is a correlation between reproducibility scores and citation context sentiment in a pilot dataset of ML papers.
ML models can be trained to classify citation contexts into reproducibility sentiments with reasonable accuracy.
limitations: The study is based on a relatively small pilot dataset of 22 papers and 1,937 citation contexts.
The correlation observed may not be generalizable to all ML papers or other fields.

result_claims.metric: correlation between reproducibility scores and citation context sentiment

partial_results.metric: correlation between reproducibility scores and citation context sentiment
partial_results.note: The correlation is based on a pilot dataset and may not generalize to the broader ML literature.

sun2023_ranking_mobility.title: Ranking mobility and impact inequality in early academic careers
sun2023_ranking_mobility.research_question: How difficult is it for an early career academic to climb the ranks of their discipline?
conclusion_claims: Ranking mobility is the lowest for the top and bottom-ranked authors, indicating a high degree of stability in these strata.
Mobility of impact rankings has increased over time, accompanied by a decline in impact inequality, suggesting a more equitable distribution of academic impact.
limitations: Citations are used as a proxy for academic impact, which may not fully capture the broader impact of research.
The analysis is limited to authors whose careers began between 1986 and 2008, which may not reflect current trends.

result_claims.value: remarkably low for top and bottom-ranked authors

partial_results.note: The results vary across disciplines, with some showing increased stability in the top 10% and others showing increased mobility in the bottom 10%.

vasarhelyi2023_who_benefits.title: Who benefits from altmetrics? The effect of team gender composition on the link between online visibility and citation impact
vasarhelyi2023_who_benefits.research_question: Does online visibility help female researchers overcome the gender-based citation penalty, and how does team gender composition interact with online visibility in affecting citation impact across different research areas?
vasarhelyi2023_who_benefits.data_source: Web of Science, Altmetric, Open Academic Graph
conclusion_claims: Online visibility positively affects citation impact across research areas.
Team gender composition interacts differently with online visibility in different research areas, suggesting the need for area-specific strategies to promote citation equity.
limitations: The study is limited to three broad research areas (Computer Science, Engineering, and Social Sciences).
The use of name-based algorithmic gender inference may introduce errors, although the authors devised a method to emulate and account for these errors.

result_claims.value: Citation benefit for teams with female first and/or last authors

partial_results.note: The result depends on the research area, which invites discussions about custom strategies to promote citation equity.

deng2023_enhancing_disruption.title: Enhancing the robustness of the disruption metric against noise
deng2023_enhancing_disruption.research_question: How can the disruption metric be improved to better distinguish innovative papers from developmental ones and increase its robustness against noise from highly cited references?
conclusion_claims: The modified disruption metric better distinguishes innovative papers from developmental ones.
The new disruption metric is more robust against the influence of highly cited references and random link removal.
limitations: The dataset is limited to physics papers from the American Physical Society.
The paper does not provide a statistical justification for the optimal hot-spot elimination percentile.

result_claims.metric: average rank of Nobel prize winning papers
Kendall’s τ between original and new ranks
result_claims.value: decreases with higher fraction of hot-spot papers eliminated

partial_results.note: The optimal fraction is 3%, but the paper does not provide a statistical justification for this value.

conclusion_claims: Preprints play a critical role in accelerating innovation by expediting the dissemination of scientific knowledge into technological innovation.

limitations: The study focuses only on bioRxiv preprints and may not generalize to other preprint servers.
The analysis is descriptive and does not include statistical modeling to test the significance of observed trends.

Preprints expedite the dissemination of scientific knowledge into technological innovation.

maddi2024_peer_review_reports.title: On the peer review reports: does size matter?
maddi2024_peer_review_reports.research_question: Does the length of peer review reports influence the citation impact of scholarly publications?
conclusion_claims: Longer peer review reports are significantly associated with higher citation impact, suggesting that they contribute to the quality and visibility of scholarly publications.
The study highlights the importance of timely and comprehensive peer review reports and suggests that open access to reports may encourage more detailed reviews.
limitations: Citations are a contested proxy for quality, as they can be influenced by sociological, strategic, and stochastic factors.
The study does not account for the content or quality of the peer review reports, only their length.

partial_results.note: The relationship between the length of peer review reports and citation impact is observed, but the study acknowledges that citations are influenced by sociological and strategic factors.

zheng2025_male_female_retractions.title: Do male leading authors retract more articles than female leading authors?
zheng2025_male_female_retractions.research_question: Do male leading (i.e., first and corresponding) authors have a higher rate of article retractions compared to female leading authors, relative to their research productivity?
available_fields: gender of first and corresponding authors
conclusion_claims: Male first authors have higher retraction rates, particularly for scientific misconduct such as plagiarism, authorship disputes, ethical issues, duplication, and fabrication/falsification.
Understanding these gendered patterns of retraction may contribute to strategies aimed at reducing their prevalence.
limitations: The study is based on self-reported or publicly available gender data, which may not be fully accurate.
The dataset may not capture all retracted articles, as some retractions may not be indexed in Web of Science or Retraction Watch.

result_claims.value: Higher for male first authors in misconduct-related reasons (plagiarism, authorship disputes, ethical issues, duplication, fabrication/falsification)
Higher for male first authors in biomedical and health sciences, life and earth sciences; higher for female first authors in mathematics and computer science

partial_results.note: No significant gender differences were found in retractions attributed to mistakes.

zheng2025_social_media_retraction.title: Can social media provide early warning of retraction? Evidence from critical tweets identified by human annotation and large language models
zheng2025_social_media_retraction.venue: Journal of the Association for Information Science and Technology
zheng2025_social_media_retraction.research_question: Can social media commentary, specifically critical tweets, serve as an early warning signal for scholarly articles that are later retracted?
zheng2025_social_media_retraction.data_source: Twitter, Crossref, and journal retraction databases
conclusion_claims: Critical tweets identified by human annotation are more common for retracted articles than for non-retracted articles, suggesting their potential as early warning signals.
A human-AI collaborative approach may offer a more reliable and scalable method for monitoring post-publication discourse and identifying problematic research.
limitations: The study relies on a relatively small sample of retracted and non-retracted articles.
The use of LLMs for identifying critical tweets may introduce noise or miss context, highlighting the need for human oversight.

result_claims.metric: Proportion of retracted articles with critical tweets
Proportion of non-retracted articles with critical tweets
Alignment between human annotation and LLMs

partial_results.metric: LLM performance in identifying critical tweets
partial_results.note: LLMs only partially aligned with human annotation, suggesting limitations in fully automated monitoring.

donner2024_data_inaccuracy.title: Data inaccuracy quantification and uncertainty propagation for bibliometric indicators
donner2024_data_inaccuracy.research_question: How can uncertainty in bibliometric indicator values be quantified and propagated due to data inaccuracies?
donner2024_data_inaccuracy.data_source: Empirical data samples and real bibliometric research evaluation data
conclusion_claims: Uncertainty in bibliometric indicator values due to data errors can be quantified using Bayesian regression models and Monte Carlo simulation.
Citation impact scores of research groups often have large margins of error due to data inaccuracies and should be used with caution.
limitations: Only two out of many known bibliometric error categories are considered, leading to an underestimation of total uncertainty.
The method does not account for all sources of data inaccuracies.

partial_results.note: Only two out of many known bibliometric error categories are considered, so the results likely underestimate the full uncertainty

suebsombut2023_smart_farm.data_source: Web of Science (WOS) and Scopus online database
conclusion_claims: The major cluster of smart farm research is the soil carbon emission from farming activity, which impacts on climate change that affects food production and productivity.
The contribution is to identify the trends on smart farm to develop research in the future by means of bibliometric analysis.
limitations: The study does not provide detailed statistical significance for the identified research clusters.

result_claims.value: soil carbon emission from farming activity

partial_results.note: The study identifies two clusters but does not provide detailed statistical significance for the clusters.

fletcher2024_reproducibility.title: Reproducibility study of “LICO: Explainable Models with Language-Image Consistency”
fletcher2024_reproducibility.research_question: Can the claims made by Lei et al. (2023) regarding the LICO method be reproduced, particularly in terms of improved classification performance and interpretability?
fletcher2024_reproducibility.data_source: CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and subsets of ImageNet
conclusion_claims: The claims made by Lei et al. (2023) regarding the LICO method could not be consistently reproduced.
The study highlights the importance of rigorous evaluation and transparent reporting in interpretability research.
limitations: The original codebase was incomplete, requiring manual implementation of certain components.
The study focused on a subset of the original experiments, which may not fully capture the scope of the LICO method.

partial_results.note: The claim that tasks with more classification categories benefit more from LICO could not be fully evaluated due to incomplete data.

hanson2023_strain_publishing.title: The strain on scientific publishing
hanson2023_strain_publishing.research_question: What factors are contributing to the strain on scientific publishing, and how can this strain be addressed?
hanson2023_strain_publishing.data_source: Scopus, Web of Science, web scrapes, and direct requests to publishers
conclusion_claims: The exponential growth in scientific publications is outpacing the growth in the number of practicing scientists, leading to a strain on the publishing system.
The strain on scientific publishing is not strictly tied to any one publisher business model, although some behaviors are associated with specific gold open access publishers.
The metrics introduced in this study can help inform actionable solutions to address the strain on scientific publishing.
limitations: The study focuses on journals indexed in both Scopus and Web of Science, which may not represent the full spectrum of scientific publishing.
The analysis is primarily descriptive and does not establish causal relationships between the identified metrics and the strain on publishing.

result_claims.value: disproportionate growth in articles published in special issues
reduced turnaround times for articles in special issues
widespread year-over-year inflation of journal impact factors

partial_results.note: The relationship between impact inflation and quality signals is not fully established and requires further investigation.

lin2023_moprd.title: MOPRD: A multidisciplinary open peer review dataset
lin2023_moprd.research_question: How can a multidisciplinary open peer review dataset be constructed to support a wide range of peer review-related research and applications?
lin2023_moprd.data_source: Data were collected from journals across different disciplines using special crawlers with native and auto-labeling labels.
conclusion_claims: MOPRD is a multidisciplinary open peer review dataset that covers the entire peer review process and supports a wide range of research and applications.
The modular guided review comment generation method based on MOPRD delivers better performance compared to existing methods.
limitations: The paper does not provide specific details on the size of the dataset.
The paper does not provide detailed results for all explored applications, such as editorial decision prediction.

result_claims.significance: Indicated by both automatic metrics and human evaluation

partial_results.note: The paper explores this application but does not provide specific results.

sjogarde2023_covid_citations.title: The effect of the rapid growth of covid-19 publications on citation indicators
sjogarde2023_covid_citations.research_question: How does the rapid growth of covid-19 publications influence commonly used bibliometric indicators such as field-normalized citation scores and the journal impact factor?
conclusion_claims: The rapid growth of covid-19 publications affects field-normalized citation scores and the journal impact factor.

There is a considerable risk of drawing misleading conclusions from citation indicators spanning over the beginning of the covid-19 pandemic, especially when time series are used and when assessing the biomedical literature.
limitations: The study is limited to biomedical literature and does not consider the impact on other research fields.
The study does not provide a detailed analysis of the long-term effects of the pandemic on bibliometric indicators.

result_claims.value: Covid-19 publications cite recent publications to a much higher extent than other medical publications.
The inclusion of covid-19 publications more than doubles the denominator in field normalization calculations, reducing the normalized citation score of non-covid-19 publications.
Covid-19 publications are expected to affect the journal impact factor due to their high citation rates.

partial_results.note: The exact impact on the journal impact factor is not quantified in the paper.

Fields: citations, publication year, journal category, field-normalized citation score, journal impact factor

fahimifar2022_unreliable_shoulders.title: Are We Standing on Unreliable Shoulders? The Effect of Retracted Papers Citations on Previous and Subsequent Published Papers: A Study of the Web of Science Database
fahimifar2022_unreliable_shoulders.venue: International Journal of Information Science and Management
fahimifar2022_unreliable_shoulders.research_question: What is the effect of retracted papers on the citations and subsequent publications in the Web of Science database?
conclusion_claims: Retracted papers are cited differently compared to unretracted papers, indicating a potential influence on subsequent research.
Citations to retracted papers continue despite their retraction status, suggesting a need for improved monitoring by citation databases.
limitations: The study is limited to the Web of Science database and may not represent all retracted papers globally.
The analysis is descriptive and does not establish causal relationships between retraction and citation behavior.

partial_results.metric: Correlation between journal quartiles and retraction rates
partial_results.note: The correlation was not explicitly tested in the provided text.

momeni2023_factors_oa.title: Which Factors are associated with Open Access Publishing? A Springer Nature Case Study
momeni2023_factors_oa.research_question: What factors are associated with Open Access publishing, and how do these factors influence the choice of publishing model and citation impact?
conclusion_claims: Authors eligible for APC waivers are more likely to publish in gold-OA journals, suggesting that financial support is a key factor in OA publishing decisions.

The study highlights the potential for OA publishing to exacerbate existing inequalities in the scholarly communication system, particularly for authors from low-income countries or those without financial support.
limitations: The study is limited to articles published by Springer Nature, which may not be representative of the broader scholarly publishing landscape.
The study does not fully disentangle the effects of OA from other factors that may influence citation impact, such as article quality or journal prestige.

result_claims.value: Authors eligible for APC waivers publish more in gold-OA journals than others
Authors eligible for an APC discount have the lowest ratio of OA publications
There is a strong correlation between journal rank and the publishing model in gold-OA journals
The OA option is mostly avoided in hybrid journals
Countries' income level, seniority, and experience with OA publications are the most predictive factors for OA publishing in hybrid journals

partial_results.note: The study notes that OA articles have a citation advantage, but the extent to which this is due to OA versus other factors (e.g., quality bias) is not fully resolved.

momeni2023_factors_oa.blind_idea:

Data available: The study analyzed 522,411 articles published by Springer Nature. The dataset includes information on authors' affiliations, country income levels, publishing models (gold OA, hybrid, closed access), and citation impact.
Fields: country income level, publishing model, citation impact, APC waiver eligibility, journal rank, author seniority, experience with OA

tiyavorabun2022_badder_seeds.title: [Re] Badder Seeds: Reproducing the Evaluation of Lexical Methods for Bias Measurement
tiyavorabun2022_badder_seeds.research_question: Can the findings of Antoniak and Mimno regarding the fragility of seed sets in bias measurement be reproduced, and are their conclusions valid?
tiyavorabun2022_badder_seeds.data_source: New York Times articles, WikiText-103, Goodreads book reviews, GoogleNews word2vec model
conclusion_claims: Seed sets used for bias measurement often suffer from biases that affect their performance as a baseline for bias metrics.
The fragility of seed sets is confirmed, supporting the need for better justification and analysis of seed sets in future work.
limitations: Lack of publicly available code and documentation from the original paper made the replication process challenging.
Minor discrepancies in preprocessing statistics due to implementation differences.

partial_results.note: Unable to reproduce vocabulary size accurately due to implementation differences.

ghorbi2023_iranian_retractions.title: Retracted papers by Iranian authors: Causes, journals, time lags, affiliations, collaborations
ghorbi2023_iranian_retractions.research_question: What are the causes, journals, time lags, affiliations, and collaborations associated with retracted papers by Iranian authors?
ghorbi2023_iranian_retractions.data_source: Scopus database and Retraction Watch database
conclusion_claims: The peer review system appears to be a weak point in the submission/publication process, and improving it would likely reduce the number of retractions.

Iranian institutions, particularly Islamic Azad University, are disproportionately represented in retracted papers, suggesting a need for improved oversight and ethical standards in Iranian academic publishing.
limitations: The study is limited to retracted papers indexed in Scopus and Retraction Watch, which may not capture all retracted papers by Iranian authors.
The study does not provide a detailed analysis of the underlying causes of fake peer review and plagiarism, such as institutional pressures or individual motivations.

partial_results.note: The study does not provide a detailed breakdown of the number of retractions for reasons other than fake peer review, plagiarism, and duplicate publication.

Fields: retraction reasons, journals, time lags, affiliations, collaborations

rudnicka2021_in_order_that.title: In order that – a data driven study of symptoms and causes of obsolescence
rudnicka2021_in_order_that.research_question: What are the symptoms and causes of the obsolescence of the English purpose subordinator 'in order that'?
rudnicka2021_in_order_that.data_source: Corpus of Historical American English (COHA), Corpus of Contemporary American English (COCA)
conclusion_claims: The purpose subordinator 'in order that' is showing clear symptoms of obsolescence, including a strong negative correlation between time and frequency of use and increased concentration in formal genres.
The obsolescence of 'in order that' is influenced by both external (socio-cultural changes) and internal (rise of the to-infinitive) higher-order processes.
limitations: The study is limited to the English language and the American English corpus, which may not generalize to other varieties of English or other languages.
The extrapolation method used to adjust for genre distribution may introduce some bias.

partial_results.note: Although 'in order that' is found in spoken language, its frequency is much lower compared to written language.

rudnicka2021_in_order_that.blind_idea:

Fields: genre, register, frequency, time_period

okamura2022_scientometric_engineering.title: Scientometric engineering: Exploring citation dynamics via arXiv eprints
okamura2022_scientometric_engineering.research_question: How has the shift to preprint servers like arXiv affected the citation dynamics and attention patterns in different scientific disciplines?
conclusion_claims: The citation dynamics of arXiv eprints vary significantly across disciplines, reflecting different publication and communication practices.
The development of a normalized citation index and a stochastic model provides a new framework for understanding citation dynamics in the context of preprint servers.
limitations: The study is limited to arXiv eprints and may not generalize to other preprint servers or traditional journal publications.
The analysis does not account for the quality or impact of the cited works beyond citation counts.

partial_results.note: Results may vary depending on the specific subfield within a discipline.

kerzendorf2020_distributed_peer_review.title: Distributed peer review enhanced with natural language processing and machine learning
kerzendorf2020_distributed_peer_review.research_question: Does the distributed peer review system, enhanced with NLP and machine learning, produce outcomes comparable to traditional peer review, and how do factors like reviewer expertise and seniority influence review quality?
kerzendorf2020_distributed_peer_review.data_source: European Southern Observatory (ESO) proposal data from Period 103 (2018), including volunteer submissions and reviews.
conclusion_claims: The distributed peer review system is statistically equivalent to traditional peer review in terms of proposal ranking.
The machine learning algorithm can predict reviewer expertise with a high success rate.
Seniority and reviewer expertise influence the quality of reviews.
limitations: The experiment was voluntary and did not affect actual telescope time allocation.
The results are based on a relatively small sample size (172 proposals).
The study does not provide detailed metrics for the success rate of the machine learning algorithm.

result_claims.value: 40% for first quartile, 30% for second and third quartiles

partial_results.note: The success rate is described as 'high' but no specific metrics are provided.

chaieb2022_data_obsolescence.title: Data Obsolescence Detection in the Light of Newly Acquired Valid Observations
chaieb2022_data_obsolescence.research_question: How can we detect and explain obsolete information in a database when new valid observations contradict existing ones?
chaieb2022_data_obsolescence.data_source: Elderly database with sensor data for fall-prevention monitoring
conclusion_claims: The proposed causation-based system effectively detects contradictions between new and existing observations.
The AND-OR tree explanation method provides reliable recommendations for updating obsolete information in a database.
limitations: The paper does not provide a detailed evaluation of the optimal threshold for ǫ-Contradiction.
The approach is demonstrated in a single real-life application (elderly fall-prevention), and its generalizability to other domains is not fully explored.

demonstrated in a real-life medical application
demonstrated in a real-life medical application

partial_results.note: The optimal threshold for detecting contradictions is not fully evaluated in the paper.

xia2019_function_point.title: Updating Weight Values for Function Point Counting
xia2019_function_point.venue: International Journal of Hybrid Intelligent Systems
xia2019_function_point.research_question: How can the outdated weight values in Function Point counting be updated to improve the accuracy of software effort estimation?
conclusion_claims: The proposed neuro-fuzzy model significantly improves the accuracy of software effort estimation compared to traditional FP methods.
The integration of neural networks and fuzzy logic effectively captures the uncertainties and imprecisions in software development.
limitations: The calibration was done using a limited dataset, which may affect the generality of the results.
The model's performance may vary with different types of software projects and data distributions.

result_claims.significance: Empirical validation shows an average improvement of 22% in the accuracy of software effort estimations.

partial_results.note: The correlation is still unsatisfactory due to the wide variation of data points with many outliers.

arroyomachado2020_science_wikipedia.title: Science through Wikipedia: a novel representation of open knowledge through co-citation networks
arroyomachado2020_science_wikipedia.research_question: How does Wikipedia represent scientific knowledge, and what patterns emerge in the citation of scientific literature through this open encyclopaedia?
arroyomachado2020_science_wikipedia.data_source: Altmetric.com, Elsevier's CiteScore Metrics, Scopus ASJC
conclusion_claims: Wikipedia provides a significant representation of scientific knowledge, with a strong presence of Medicine and Biochemistry, Genetics and Molecular Biology.
High-impact factor journals are more likely to be cited in Wikipedia, suggesting that visibility and impact are key factors in citation practices.
limitations: The study focuses on English Wikipedia, which may not represent the global perspective of scientific knowledge.
The analysis is based on citations to peer-reviewed articles, excluding other types of scientific sources.

partial_results.note: Only 5.49% of Wikipedia citations correspond to the Humanities, which is not the focus of this study.

zhao2023_effect_of_gender_diversity.title: The Effect of Gender Diversity on Scientific Team Impact: A Team Roles Perspective
zhao2023_effect_of_gender_diversity.research_question: How does gender diversity within different team roles affect the team’s impact, and can team size moderate this relationship?
conclusion_claims: The relationship between gender diversity and team impact follows an inverted U-shape for both leadership and support groups.
Teams with an all-female leadership group and an all-male support group achieve higher impact than other team types.
limitations: The study focuses primarily on biomedical-related disciplines, which may limit generalizability to other fields.
The use of author contribution statements may not fully capture the complexity of role differentiation in all teams.

result_claims.value: negative for small teams, positive for large teams

partial_results.note: The effect of leadership-group gender diversity becomes positive and statistically insignificant in large teams.

Ribeiro2026_GenderProductivityPrestige.title: Gender shapes the relationship between productivity and journal prestige in science
Ribeiro2026_GenderProductivityPrestige.research_question: How does gender shape the relationship between research productivity and journal prestige in science?
available_fields: Average journal prestige (Journal Impact Factor and SCImago Journal Rank)
conclusion_claims: Male researchers tend to adopt productivity-driven strategies, while female researchers more often pursue impact-oriented trajectories.
Gender-sensitive approaches in research evaluation are essential to foster a more equitable, fair, and efficient academic environment.
limitations: The study focuses on a specific national context (Brazil), which may limit generalizability to other academic systems.
The dataset is based on self-reported information from the Lattes platform, which may introduce potential biases.

result_claims.value: Comparable or higher for female researchers, particularly in later career stages and among outlier individuals

result_claims.metric: Temporal persistence in productivity and impact
Negative association between productivity and average journal prestige
result_claims.value: Observed for both genders, with slightly stronger effects among female researchers

partial_results.note: Patterns vary across disciplines, highlighting the complexity and heterogeneity of academic careers.

banerjee2025_citation_diversity.title: Exploring Citation Diversity in Scholarly Literature: An Entropy-Based Approach
banerjee2025_citation_diversity.research_question: How does citation diversity vary across countries, academic disciplines, and demographic factors such as gender, and what does this reveal about the structure of scholarly influence?
conclusion_claims: The entropy-based citation diversity measure provides deeper insights into the unevenness of citation distributions compared to traditional citation counts.
Citation diversity varies significantly across countries, disciplines, and genders, highlighting the need for a more equitable view of academic achievements.
limitations: The study is limited to top-ranked institutions and award-winning scientists, which may not represent the broader academic population.
The analysis is based on a single year of data, which may not capture long-term trends in citation diversity.

result_claims.value: Varies significantly across countries and disciplines
Countries with small and large economies cluster similarly
Varies between male and female scientists

partial_results.note: The composition of countries within each group changes as the number of top institutions considered in the analysis varies.

nakajima2023_quantifying_gender_imbalanc.research_question: What is the extent of gender imbalance in East Asian academia in terms of the number of researchers, their research careers, and citation practices, and how does it compare to other countries?
conclusion_claims: China, Japan, and South Korea exhibit larger gender imbalance in academia compared to other research-active countries in terms of the number of researchers and citation practices.

limitations: The difficulty of inferring gender from Asian names may affect the accuracy of the analysis.

gong2023_citation_advantage.title: The citation advantage of foreign language references for Chinese social science papers
gong2023_citation_advantage.research_question: Does the inclusion of foreign language references in Chinese social science papers lead to a citation advantage, and how does this advantage evolve over time?
conclusion_claims: Incorporating foreign language literature continues to increase scientific impact in Chinese social sciences, even as the scientific community becomes increasingly international.

The citation advantage of foreign language references remains significant after controlling for paper characteristics such as discipline, journal prestige, institution prestige, and scientific collaboration.
limitations: The study is limited to Chinese social science papers indexed in CSSCI and does not include international publications.
The analysis does not account for the quality of the cited foreign language references.

result_claims.value: Papers with foreign language references receive significantly more citations than those without
The citation advantage decreased from 1998 to 2008 but stabilized after 2008

partial_results.note: The decline of the citation advantage from 1998 to 2008 is largely an artifact of the increased number of papers citing foreign language material.

doukhan2024_voice_passing.title: Voice Passing: a Non-Binary Voice Gender Prediction System for evaluating Transgender voice transition
doukhan2024_voice_passing.research_question: How can a non-binary voice gender prediction system be developed and evaluated to better reflect human perception of gender in transgender voices?
doukhan2024_voice_passing.data_source: A corpus of 41 French cis- and transgender speakers, as well as external gender-balanced data for training models.
conclusion_claims: The VFP index provides a continuous measure of voice femininity that better reflects human perception of gender than traditional binary classification.
The accuracy of VFP estimation is influenced by factors such as speaking style, age, and the conception of gender as binary or non-binary.
limitations: The study is limited to the French language and culture, so the results may not generalize to other contexts.
The sample size is relatively small, with only 41 speakers, which may limit the statistical power of the analysis.

Proportion of 'Male' answers for Cisgender Males (CM)
Proportion of 'IDK' answers for Transgender Females (TF)
Mean Reaction Time (RT) for Transgender Females (TF)

result_claims.value: Higher than F0 or VTL-based models

result_claims.metric: Effect of age on model accuracy

partial_results.metric: Effect of speaking style on VFP estimation
partial_results.note: The impact of speaking style on VFP estimation is mentioned but not quantified in the provided text.

abramo2015_research_performance.title: Should the research performance of scientists be distinguished by gender?
abramo2015_research_performance.research_question: Does separating the measurement of research performance by gender produce notably different results compared to measurement without such distinction?
abramo2015_research_performance.data_source: Italian Ministry of Education, Universities and Research (MIUR) data on academic staff and research performance in hard sciences.
conclusion_claims: Distinguishing research performance by gender can lead to different ranking outcomes compared to evaluations without gender distinction.
In contexts where gender discrimination or family roles affect research productivity, evaluations without gender distinction may penalize female researchers.
limitations: The study is limited to the Italian academic system and hard sciences, which may not generalize to other contexts.
The analysis does not account for personal and environmental factors that may influence research productivity.

result_claims.value: Female researchers show lower productivity compared to male researchers.
Ranking lists with gender distinction show different relative positions for female researchers compared to evaluations without gender distinction.

partial_results.note: The extent of differences may vary across research fields and career stages, which is not fully explored in this study.

bezerra2025_leveraging_gans.research_question: How does leveraging GANs for citation intent classification impact the performance of intent classification and the centrality of papers in citation networks?
conclusion_claims: The proposed GAN-based method achieves competitive performance in citation intent classification with fewer parameters.
Filtering citations by intent significantly affects the centrality of papers in citation networks, particularly for betweenness centrality.
limitations: Lower performance on the 3C dataset suggests potential limitations in generalization.
The study focuses on a specific set of citation intent categories, which may not capture the full complexity of citation functions.

result_claims.value: Most sensitive to citation intent filtering

partial_results.note: Lower performance on the 3C dataset compared to other benchmarks, indicating potential limitations in generalization.

li2023_two_stage.research_question: How can dynamic creative optimization be effectively implemented in e-commerce advertising when dealing with sparse and ambiguous samples?
conclusion_claims: The proposed two-stage dynamic creative optimization framework outperforms existing methods in terms of click-through rate.
The use of a transformer-based rerank model and distillation method improves the model's ability to handle ambiguous samples.
limitations: The model's performance is dependent on the quality and quantity of historical data.
The framework may not generalize well to other domains outside of e-commerce advertising.

partial_results.note: The ranking accuracy of the autoco model is limited by the ambiguity of the samples.

baccini2025_similarity_matrix.title: Similarity matrix average for aggregating multiplex networks
baccini2025_similarity_matrix.research_question: How can the layers of a multiplex network be aggregated into a single monoplex network using similarity matrix averaging?
baccini2025_similarity_matrix.data_source: Multiplex similarity network of statistical journals
conclusion_claims: The proposed methodology based on similarity matrix averaging provides a valid way to aggregate the layers of a multiplex network into a single monoplex network.
The Fréchet mean criterion with different metrics can be used to define appropriate averages for positive definite matrices in multiplex networks.
limitations: The paper does not provide specific numerical results or empirical validation of the proposed methodology.
The application to the multiplex network of statistical journals is not detailed in the abstract.

partial_results.note: The specific values of similarity for each layer are not provided in the abstract.

diBona2023_decentralization.title: The Concept of Decentralization Through Time and Disciplines: A Quantitative Exploration
diBona2023_decentralization.research_question: Have different disciplines independently developed the concept of (de)centralization, and how have they influenced each other over time?
conclusion_claims: The concept of (de)centralization has independently emerged in different academic fields with minimal cross-disciplinary influence.
Blockchain has become the most influential and productive field regarding (de)centralization in the past 10 years.
limitations: The dataset includes some unrelated articles due to the use of the root string 'centrali'.
The clustering method may not capture all nuances of the concept's evolution.

result_claims.value: Most influential field in the past 10 years

partial_results.note: Not explicitly quantified in the paper, but inferred from clustering results.

asatani2025_influential_scientists.title: Influential scientists shape knowledge flows between science and IGO policy
asatani2025_influential_scientists.research_question: What are the mechanisms through which scientific research is translated into IGO policy, and who are the key individuals and networks that dominate this process?
asatani2025_influential_scientists.data_source: Overton, Scopus, and IGO policy documents
conclusion_claims: A small group of policy-influential scientists (PI-Sci) dominate the flow of scientific knowledge into IGO policy, particularly in established fields.
The structure of co-authorship networks and the prior policy influence of authors are key factors in determining the likelihood of policy citation.
limitations: The study focuses on IGO policy documents and may not capture the full scope of science-policy interactions in other contexts.
The analysis is based on citation data, which may not fully reflect the actual impact of scientific research on policy decisions.

result_claims.value: 93.3% of papers are connected to IGO policy documents within 3 or 4 steps in the citation network.
Papers citing more than eight policy-cited references have a 30% chance of subsequent policy citation.
A small group of scientists (PI-Sci) receive a disproportionate share of policy citations and collaborate within dense international networks.

partial_results.metric: Concentration of influence in emerging fields
partial_results.note: The concentration of influence is weaker in rapidly evolving areas like data science and AI compared to established fields like climate modeling.

fitzgerald2021_localization.title: Is academia becoming more localised? The growth of regional knowledge networks within international research collaboration
fitzgerald2021_localization.research_question: Is there an increasing trend in regional knowledge networks within international research collaboration, and how do these networks align with historical or geographical factors?
conclusion_claims: International research collaboration has become more localized over the past five decades, with an increase in within-region collaboration relative to international collaboration.
The alignment of collaboration networks with geographical regions has increased since 2000, while historical geopolitical or colonial lines have become less relevant.
limitations: The study is based on Scopus data, which may not capture all scientific publications, especially from non-English or non-Western sources.
The analysis does not provide a detailed breakdown of collaboration patterns by specific disciplines.

partial_results.note: The paper does not provide detailed disciplinary breakdowns of the results.

nikolentzos2021_author_collaboration.research_question: Can the collaboration patterns of an author predict their future ℎ-index?
conclusion_claims: The structural role of an author in the co-authorship network is empirically related to their future ℎ-index.
Graph neural networks outperform traditional machine learning methods in predicting the ℎ-index.
limitations: The study is limited to the field of computer science.
The predictive power of textual features is limited.

result_claims.metric: relationship between structural role and future ℎ-index

partial_results.note: Textual features generated from the author's published papers do not provide much information.

mongeon2023_concentration.title: Concentration of research funding leads to decreasing marginal returns
mongeon2023_concentration.research_question: How do research productivity and scientific impact of individual researchers vary with the amount of funding they receive, and is this variation similar across different scientific fields?
mongeon2023_concentration.data_source: Funding data from research councils in Québec, combined with publication and citation data.
conclusion_claims: The concentration of research funding in the hands of a few researchers generally produces diminishing marginal returns in terms of scientific output and impact.
The most funded researchers do not necessarily produce the highest scientific output or impact.
limitations: The study is limited to researchers in Québec and may not be generalizable to other regions or countries.
The study does not account for all potential confounding factors that may influence scientific output and impact.

result_claims.value: do not stand out in terms of output and impact

partial_results.note: The study does not provide detailed results on how the relationship between funding and output/impact varies across specific fields.

nakajima2023_higher_order_rich_club.title: Higher-order rich-club phenomenon in collaborative research grant networks
nakajima2023_higher_order_rich_club.research_question: Do institutions participating in many collaborative grants gain advantages in their per-dollar research impact when they densely collaborate with each other (i.e., they form a rich club) in research grants?
conclusion_claims: Institutions that form rich clubs in collaborative grant networks tend to have higher per-dollar research productivity and impact.
Collaborative grants within rich clubs are more productive and impactful in a per-dollar sense compared to those outside of rich clubs.
limitations: The study is limited to US-based institutions and NSF-funded grants, which may not generalize to other regions or funding agencies.
The analysis does not account for unobserved factors that may influence research productivity and impact, such as institutional resources or researcher expertise.

partial_results.note: The relationship between collaboration size and productivity/impact is not fully quantified in the summary.

tohalino2021_predicting_grants.title: On predicting research grants productivity
tohalino2021_predicting_grants.research_question: Can bibliometric features extracted from researchers, institutions, and publications predict the productivity of research grants?
tohalino2021_predicting_grants.data_source: São Paulo Research Foundation (FAPESP) dataset, retrieved from Biblioteca Virtual website
conclusion_claims: Research subject and publication history are significant predictors of grant productivity.
Institution-based features contribute to predicting grant success when combined with other features.
Machine learning models outperformed text-based attributes in predicting grant productivity, but the overall accuracy was not very high.
limitations: The evaluated features were not highly discriminative.
The study focused on a specific set of bibliometric features, which may not capture all relevant factors influencing grant success.
The dataset is limited to grants from FAPESP in Brazil, which may limit generalizability.

result_claims.value: research subject and publication history were the most effective predictors
SVM and MLP yielded the highest accuracy rates

partial_results.note: Typical prediction accuracy was not very high, though higher than approaches based on textual features alone.

gross2019_contest_models.title: Contest models highlight inherent inefficiencies of scientific funding competitions
gross2019_contest_models.research_question: To what extent does the community’s investment in proposal preparation negate the scientific impact of the funding program? Are there alternative mechanisms for awarding funds that advance science more efficiently?
conclusion_claims: The effort researchers waste in writing proposals may be comparable to the total scientific value of the research that the funding supports, especially when only a few proposals can be funded.
Lost efficiency may be restored either by partial lotteries for funding or by funding researchers based on past scientific success instead of proposals for future work.
limitations: The model assumes a large number of scientists and predictable competition strength, which may not always hold in practice.
The model does not account for variability among reviewers' opinions in assessing proposal strength.

result_claims.value: The effort wasted in writing proposals may be comparable to the total scientific value of the research that the funding supports.

When professional pressures motivate investigators to seek funding for reasons beyond the value of the proposed science, the entire program can hamper scientific progress when the number of awards is small.

partial_results.note: The results depend on the number of proposals funded and the presence of extra-scientific incentives.

mryglod2014_is_your_epl_attractive.title: Is your EPL attractive? Classification of publications through download statistics
mryglod2014_is_your_epl_attractive.research_question: How can the attractiveness of EPL publications be classified and modeled using download statistics?
conclusion_claims: EPL papers exhibit distinct download patterns that can be classified into categories such as intense bursts of activity, high attractiveness, and delayed recognition.
Open-access papers accumulate downloads faster than non-open-access papers, but the proportions of papers in each category are similar.
limitations: Download statistics may not fully capture the impact or quality of a paper.
The analysis is limited to EPL and may not generalize to other journals.

result_claims.value: 2% of non-OA papers show intense bursts of download activity
18% of non-OA papers have an especially high degree of attractiveness
20% of papers exhibit delayed recognition with late spurs in download activity
OA papers have more downloads on average than non-OA papers

partial_results.note: The model predicts long-term download proportions, but actual data may vary.

zhao2020_delayed_recognition.title: Delayed Recognition; the Co-citation Perspective
zhao2020_delayed_recognition.research_question: Can delayed recognition be observed in co-citations, and what are the characteristics of such delayed co-citations?
conclusion_claims: Delayed recognition can be observed in co-citations, with 1,196 cases identified in the dataset.
The characteristics of delayed co-citations include a long sleeping duration and a sharp increase in co-citation frequency during the awakening period.
limitations: The study is limited to co-citations in the Scopus database and may not generalize to other citation databases.
The criteria for identifying delayed co-citations are relatively conservative and may exclude some valid cases.

partial_results.note: The study does not provide a definitive answer on whether delayed co-citations are derived from Sleeping Beauty publications.

ke2018_comparing_scientific_technologica.title: Comparing scientific and technological impact of biomedical research
ke2018_comparing_scientific_technologica.research_question: How do the scientific and technological impacts of biomedical research compare in terms of citation patterns, overlap, and temporal dynamics?
ke2018_comparing_scientific_technologica.data_source: USPTO, PubMed, Web of Science, NLM Catalog
conclusion_claims: There is a positive correlation between citations from patents and from papers, but the overlap in top-cited papers is small.
Patent citations generally lag behind paper citations by a median of 6 years, indicating a delay in technological impact.
limitations: The study focuses only on U.S. patents and biomedical papers, limiting generalizability.
The matching of non-patent references to MEDLINE papers may have some false negatives.

result_claims.metric: correlation between patent and paper citations

partial_results.metric: citation dynamics for delayed recognition papers
partial_results.note: The study identifies delayed recognition papers but does not provide a detailed statistical analysis of their characteristics.

kojaku2025_community_centric.title: Community-centric modeling of citation dynamics explains collective citation patterns in science, law, and patents
kojaku2025_community_centric.research_question: Do citation patterns in science, law, and patents exhibit universal statistical properties, and can a unified model explain these patterns and phenomena like delayed recognition?
kojaku2025_community_centric.data_source: SciSciNet, American Physical Society (APS) dataset, United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO), and U.S. federal judicial opinions.
conclusion_claims: Citation patterns in science, law, and patents exhibit remarkably similar statistical properties, including preferential attachment, recency bias, and delayed recognition.
The proposed Community Citation Model (CCM) captures the core mechanisms driving these patterns and provides a unifying framework for understanding citation dynamics across knowledge systems.
limitations: The paper does not explicitly address the limitations of the model in capturing all aspects of citation dynamics.
The analysis is based on descriptive statistics and does not include formal hypothesis testing or inferential methods.

partial_results.note: The model's ability to predict future citation success is not fully quantified in the provided text.

mir2026_forsaking.title: Forsaking your own: unveiling the delayed recognition of Garfield’s work on the 'delayed recognition' phenomenon
mir2026_forsaking.research_question: Has the phenomenon of delayed recognition itself suffered delayed recognition, particularly in the case of Garfield's 1980 paper?
mir2026_forsaking.data_source: Web of Science (WoS), Scopus, and manual analysis of citation data from Current Contents (CC) magazine.
conclusion_claims: Garfield's 1980 paper on delayed recognition experienced a delayed awakening in citations, consistent with the DR phenomenon it describes.
The paper was largely overlooked for 28 years before receiving a modest increase in citations, suggesting that the concept of DR itself may have suffered delayed recognition.
limitations: The citation data for Garfield's work in Current Contents (CC) is not accurately reported in WoS and Scopus, which may affect the reliability of the citation counts.
The analysis is limited to a single paper, which may not be representative of the broader impact of Garfield's work on DR.

result_claims.value: 10 citations up to 2004, 20 citations in the next four years

partial_results.note: Citations were largely self-citations in the early years, which may have skewed the results.

chandrasekaran2021_automating_transfer.title: Automating Transfer Credit Assessment in Student Mobility - A Natural Language Processing-based Approach
chandrasekaran2021_automating_transfer.research_question: How can NLP techniques be used to automate the assessment of transfer credits between courses for student mobility?
chandrasekaran2021_automating_transfer.data_source: A new benchmark dataset containing seven course-to-course similarity measures was proposed due to the lack of quality benchmark datasets.
conclusion_claims: The proposed model effectively automates the assessment of transfer credits by leveraging NLP techniques to determine the similarity between learning outcomes.
The model provides tunable parameters to accommodate different scenarios, offering flexibility to decision-making authorities.
limitations: The lack of quality benchmark datasets necessitated the creation of a new dataset, which may limit generalizability.
The inherent complexity and domain specificity of learning outcomes may affect the model's performance.

result_claims.value: determined by aggregating semantic and taxonomic similarity

partial_results.note: The specific values or performance metrics are not provided in the text.

sunahara2023_universal_productivity.title: Universal productivity patterns in research careers
sunahara2023_universal_productivity.research_question: Do universal productivity patterns exist in research careers, and if so, what are they?
conclusion_claims: There are six universal productivity patterns in research careers: constant, u-shaped, decreasing, periodic, increasing, and canonical.
The canonical productivity pattern is the most prevalent, but productivity peaks occur more frequently around mid-career rather than early.
limitations: The study is limited to Brazilian researchers, which may affect generalizability to other countries.
The dataset does not include researchers outside the CNPq Research Productivity Fellowship, potentially introducing selection bias.

partial_results.note: The study suggests cohort effects but does not provide a detailed analysis of their impact on productivity patterns

shirouyeh2023_predicting_star_scientists.title: Predicting Star Scientists in the Field of Artificial Intelligence: A Machine Learning Approach
shirouyeh2023_predicting_star_scientists.research_question: What features can be used to predict star scientists in the field of artificial intelligence, and how do these features differentiate rising stars from their non-rising counterparts?
conclusion_claims: Rising stars in AI follow different patterns in early-career features compared to non-rising stars.
The most important features in predicting star scientists in AI are the number of articles, group discipline diversity, and weighted degree centrality.
limitations: The study does not specify the exact data sources used, which limits the reproducibility of the findings.
The specific machine learning algorithm used for prediction is not detailed, making it difficult to assess the model's performance and robustness.

partial_results.note: The specific data sources used in the study are not detailed in the provided text.
The exact machine learning algorithm used for prediction is not specified in the provided text.

abramo2013_assessing_accuracy.title: Assessing the accuracy of the h and g indexes for measuring researchers’ productivity
abramo2013_assessing_accuracy.venue: Journal of the American Society for Information Science and Technology
abramo2013_assessing_accuracy.research_question: To what extent do the h-index and g-index accurately measure individual researchers' productivity compared to the FSS?
conclusion_claims: The h-index and g-index show significant discrepancies in ranking researchers compared to the FSS, indicating that they are not fully accurate measures of productivity.
The g-index provides a more accurate measure of productivity than the h-index, but neither is as accurate as the FSS.
limitations: The study is limited to Italian academic researchers in the hard sciences, so the results may not generalize to other countries or fields.
The FSS is a newly proposed indicator, and its validity and reliability have not been widely tested in other contexts.

partial_results.note: The paper does not provide exact numerical values for rank shifts but describes them as significant.

paraskevopoulos2021_academic_wanderer.title: The academic wanderer: structure of collaboration network and relation with research performance
paraskevopoulos2021_academic_wanderer.research_question: How do the structure of co-authorship ego networks, academic performance, and academic mobility interrelate across different career stages?
conclusion_claims: There is a clear correlation between the structure of co-authorship ego networks and academic performance indices, with higher productivity and impact associated with larger networks.
International academic migrants are better at growing their networks, while domestic migrants are better at achieving higher impact through their collaborations.
limitations: The study is descriptive and does not establish causal relationships between variables.
The dataset does not include detailed information on the cognitive effort or quality of collaborations.

result_claims.metric: correlation between ego network layer sizes and academic performance
capacity bound effect on ego network size
international migrants vs. domestic migrants in ego network growth
result_claims.value: International migrants grow larger networks, domestic migrants achieve higher impact

partial_results.metric: exact mechanisms of cognitive investment in collaboration
partial_results.note: Not fully quantified due to the descriptive nature of the analysis.

li2019_achieving_competitive_advantage.title: Achieving competitive advantage in academia through early career coauthorship with top scientists
li2019_achieving_competitive_advantage.research_question: Does coauthorship with top scientists early in a junior researcher's career provide a long-term competitive advantage?
li2019_achieving_competitive_advantage.data_source: Academic publication data from four scientific disciplines (Cell Biology, Chemistry, Physics, and Neuroscience)
conclusion_claims: Junior researchers who coauthor with top scientists early in their careers enjoy a persistent competitive advantage, leading to a higher probability of becoming top scientists themselves.
The impact of coauthorship with top scientists is strongest for junior researchers affiliated with less prestigious institutions, suggesting that these institutions may have untapped potential.
limitations: The study is based on observational data, so causality cannot be definitively established.
The analysis is limited to four scientific disciplines and may not generalize to other fields.

result_claims.metric: Probability of becoming a top scientist
result_claims.value: Higher for researchers with early coauthorship with top scientists

result_claims.metric: Impact of coauthorship on researchers from less prestigious institutions
result_claims.value: Stronger for researchers from less prestigious institutions

partial_results.note: The predictive power of early coauthorship is not fully quantified in the paper.

zhao2025_novelty_measurements.title: A Review on the Novelty Measurements of Academic Papers
zhao2025_novelty_measurements.research_question: What are the current methods, tools, and datasets for measuring the novelty of academic papers, and what are the open issues in this field?
conclusion_claims: Novelty is a critical but challenging concept to measure in academic papers, with various definitions and approaches proposed in the literature.

The paper identifies limitations in current novelty measures and proposes open issues for future research, including the evaluation of interdisciplinary novelty and the development of more robust validation methods.
limitations: The paper is a review and does not present original empirical data or analysis.
It does not provide a comprehensive quantitative evaluation of the effectiveness of existing novelty measures.

slavov2022_eternalism.title: Eternalism and the problem of hyperplanes
slavov2022_eternalism.research_question: Can eternalism be defended without relying on hyperplanes of simultaneity, given the conventionality of simultaneity?
conclusion_claims: Eternalism remains a credible alternative to presentism despite the challenges posed by the conventionality of simultaneity.
There are cogent reasons to prefer eternalism over presentism, even in the absence of hyperplanes.
limitations: The paper does not provide a rigorous proof for eternalism.
The analysis is primarily philosophical and does not rely on empirical data.

partial_results.note: Lack of a rigorous proof for eternalism remains an open issue.

zhao2026_novelty_differences.title: Exploring Novelty Differences between Industry and Academia: A Knowledge Entity-centric Perspective
zhao2026_novelty_differences.research_question: Is there a difference in the novelty of scientific and technical literature between industry and academia?
zhao2026_novelty_differences.data_source: Top conference papers and the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) patent dataset
conclusion_claims: Academia demonstrates higher novelty outputs compared to industry, particularly in patents.
Academia-industry collaboration has a limited effect on enhancing the novelty of research papers but helps to enhance the novelty of patents.
limitations: The study focuses on the field of artificial intelligence and natural language processing, limiting the generalizability of the findings to other domains.
The novelty measurement is based on semantic distances in a unified semantic space, which may not capture all aspects of novelty.

result_claims.value: Academia demonstrates higher novelty outputs compared to industry
Academia-industry collaboration has a limited effect on enhancing the novelty of research papers but helps to enhance the novelty of patents

partial_results.note: The effect of collaboration on novelty is limited in research papers but significant in patents
